sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: Many people dream (1) ...... living in a foreign country.
It can be an amazing experience for those who (2) ...... the courage to leave their family and friends and settle (3) ...... in a new place.
Culture shock is the feeling we get from living in a place that is so different to where we (4) ...... up that we are not sure how to deal with it.
We can often be (5) ...... aback by some of the things we find in foreign countries.
Customs and traditions can be very different and that can sometimes make it difficult to (6) ...... on with local people and to (7) ...... friends.
They might not approve (8) ...... things you do or might object (9) ...... things you say.
You might even be banned (10) ...... doing things in another country that are perfectly legal in your own.
(11) ...... you were to move to a country such as Singapore, say, you might find some of the laws very strict.
There, people can be forced (12) ...... pay a large fine just for dropping litter.
Eventually, though, most people who live abroad (13) ...... in love with their adopted country and learn to accept its differences.
It does (14) ...... real courage to make such a big change to your life, but many people agree (15) ...... it is worth it in the end.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: My (FRIEND) ...... with Harriet is one of the most important things in my life.
Many parents complain of their children's (OBEY) ......, but I think they were probably exactly the same.
Don't listen to Mary - she's just (JEALOUS) ...... of you.
We all watched (NERVE) ...... as Mark made his speech of thanks.
Don't you think that we should make sure that (ABLE) ...... people have the same rights as everyone else?
Terry is really (ARGUE) ...... and is always looking for fights with other people.
Hasn't anyone ever told you that it's very (POLITE) ...... to interrupt when other people are talking?

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
I promised my mum that I would work hard this year. **made**
I ...... that I would work hard this year.

My parents allowed me to go out even though it was very late. **let**
My parents ...... even though it was very late.

Margaret and I have had an argument, so we're not talking to each other. **out**
Margaret and I ...... so we're not talking to each other.

Nobody approved of the new law when it was introduced. **approval**
The new law didn't ...... when it was introduced.

I didn't want to have an argument, so I changed the subject. **mood**
I wasn't ...... an argument, so I changed the subject.

Tina was depressed because she couldn't make friends at summer camp. **inability**
Tina was depressed because ...... friends at summer camp.

'Could you open the door for me, please?' said Simon. **me**
Simon ...... the door for him.

Make sure you look after your little brother while I'm out. **care**
Make sure you ...... your little brother while I'm out.
If I ...... the lottery, I'd give some of the money to each member of my family.
'Did you have an argument with Francis?' 'If you had been there, you ...... the same.'
If you see Nina on Friday, ...... her to give me a ring.
If I'd known you were coming, I ...... a cake.
Remind Tony about the party ...... he's forgotten.
'Did you have a message for Dan?' 'Tell him I'll call him on Friday if you ...... him.'
This fascinating book covers some of the most ...... crimes of the twentieth century.
The government should do more for ...... people.
Ivan tells me he really ...... himself at your barbecue last week.
I know we had an argument, but now I'd quite like to .......
Harry and Sam both denied that the fight was their .......
The curtain went up, the ...... grew silent and the actors on stage began to speak.
